advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the `sign_for_kind` doc example so `keyid`/`sig` are always bound (add the HMAC branch or an else/raise) and guard `registry.resolve(kind)` returning None.
evidence: docs/attest-substrate.md:87-91: `if policy.scheme == "sshsig":` ... `sig = sshsig.sign(...)` / `keyid = "attester@example.com"` ... then unconditionally `return dsse.encode(..., [{"keyid": keyid, "sig": sig}])`
src/rebar/attest/registry.py:83-85: `def resolve(kind: str) -> Policy | None: ... return POLICY.get(kind)` — returns None for unknown kinds, so `policy.scheme` would AttributeError
The comment at docs/attest-substrate.md:90 acknowledges the HMAC branch is unimplemented (`# (HMAC-kind signing uses the env key ...)`), leaving keyid/sig unbound on that path
advisory.finding: The `sign_for_kind` example is presented as "the pattern" but has a latent NameError/AttributeError: `sig` and `keyid` are only bound inside the `if policy.scheme == "sshsig":` branch yet used unconditionally in the final `dsse.encode(..., [{"keyid": keyid, "sig": sig}])`; and `registry.resolve(kind)` returns None for an unknown kind, so `policy.scheme` would raise AttributeError. The sample fails for the HMAC path or any unknown kind.
advisory.suggested_fix: Add an explicit `else: raise ...` (or the HMAC branch) that binds `sig`/`keyid`, and handle `policy is None` before dereferencing `policy.scheme`.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Reword the signing section heading so it does not imply a `sign(kind, body)` function exists (e.g. 'Signing an attestation for a kind (no unified sign())').
evidence: docs/attest-substrate.md:69: `## Signing an attestation for a kind (`sign(kind, body)`)`
docs/attest-substrate.md:73-74: `There is deliberately **no single `sign(kind, body)` function** in the substrate`
advisory.finding: The section heading `## Signing an attestation for a kind (`sign(kind, body)`)` names a `sign(kind, body)` function that the section body explicitly states does not (and deliberately will not) exist, which can mislead a reader scanning headings/TOC.
